data.name: User
data.description: End-user who places product orders and makes payments on the e-commerce platform

threats.title: Identity Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user by stealing login credentials then performs unauthorized transactions under the victim's account.
threats.mitigation:
- Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
- Add CAPTCHA to login forms.
- Require strong password policies.
- Apply rate limiting to block brute-force attempts.

threats.title: Transaction Fraud
threats.description: A user denies having placed an order or made a payment, causing transaction disputes and potential financial losses for the merchant.
threats.mitigation:
- Implement logging for every transaction.
- Send transaction confirmations via email.

data.name: E-Commerce Application
data.description: Core e-commerce application that manages product orders, presents payment options, processes payment requests via the payment gateway, and returns transaction confirmations to users

threats.title: Purchase Tampering
threats.description: An attacker manipulates order data via HTTP request tampering before it reaches the payment gateway, resulting in paying less than the actual price.
threats.mitigation: - Validate all prices and order details in the backend.

threats.title: Elevation of Privilege
threats.description: An attacker exploits application vulnerabilities to gain administrative access.
threats.mitigation:
- Apply the principle of least privilege across all application components.
- Perform input validation.

threats.title: Denial of Service
threats.description: An attacker floods the e-commerce application with massive request volumes or sends resource-intensive requests.
threats.mitigation:
- Implement rate limiting, load balancing, and auto-scaling.
- Use DDoS protection.

threats.title: Information Disclosure Trough Error Handling
threats.description: - The application exposes sensitive information through improperly handled error messages.
threats.mitigation: - Implement custom error pages that reveal no technical details.

data.name: Payment Gateway
data.description: Third-party payment gateway that processes payment transactions, forwards payment authorization requests to banks/financial service providers, and returns transaction results to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Payment Gateway Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker creates a fake payment page via DNS hijacking to impersonate the legitimate payment gateway.
threats.mitigation:
- Use HTTPS.
- Verify the payment gateway's certificate.

threats.title: Payment Gateway Response Tampering
threats.description: An attacker performs a man-in-the-middle attack to modify the payment gateway's response causing orders to be fulfilled without valid payment.
threats.mitigation:
- Apply digital signatures to all payment gateway responses.
- Verify signatures server-side in the backend.

threats.title: Replay Attack on Payment
threats.description: An attacker captures a valid payment request and retransmits it to create duplicate transactions.
threats.mitigation:
- Assign a unique hash on each transaction.
-  Reject expired payment requests.

data.name: Bank
data.description: Bank or financial service provider that validates and processes payment transactions (credit card, e-wallet, bank transfer)

threats.title: Response Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker forges responses from the bank/financial service provider to approve transactions that should be declined.
threats.mitigation: - Verify the bank's certificate.

data.name:
Payment and Transaction
Database
data.description: Database storing order data, transaction statuses, payment details, and audit logs

threats.title: Database Tampering
threats.description: An attacker who gains database access modifies order data.
threats.mitigation:
- Encrypt data.
- Enforce strict database access controls following the least privilege principle.
- Implement database logging.

threats.title: Information Disclosure
threats.description: Sensitive user payment data stored in the database is leaked through unauthorized access.
threats.mitigation:
- Never store raw credit card data.
- Encrypt all database backups.
- Restrict database access exclusively to the backend.

threats.title: Denial of Service
threats.description: An attacker causes database overload through resource-intensive queries or excessive database connections.
threats.mitigation:
- Apply query optimization.
- Monitor database performance and configure alerts.

data.name:
Transaction Audit
Logging
data.description: Audit log storage for all payment transaction activities.

threats.title: Audit Log Tampering
threats.description: An attacker who gains system access modifies audit logs to cover traces of malicious activity.
threats.mitigation:
- Store logs in append-only (immutable) storage.
- Apply digital signatures to verify each log entry.

data.name: Query and Update Transaction
data.description: E-Commerce Application reads and updates order data and transaction statuses in the database

threats.title: SQL Injection
threats.description: An attacker injects malicious SQL commands through unvalidated input.
threats.mitigation:
- Apply strict input validation and sanitization.
- Use an ORM.
- Restrict database user privileges to only the minimum required operations.

data.name: Write Audit Log
data.description: Payment Gateway writes an audit log entry for every processed transaction

data.name: Payment Verification
data.description: E-commerce application sends payment confirmation results and updates the order status back to the user

threats.title: Payment Confirmation Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker sends a fake payment confirmation to the user.
threats.mitigation:
- Send confirmations only through verified official email.
- Include unique transaction details known only to the user and the system.

data.name:
Transaction
Result
data.description: Payment Gateway sends transaction results (success/failure, transaction ID, details) back to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker sends a forged HTTP request to the e-commerce endpoint, faking a successful payment notification from the payment gateway.
threats.mitigation:
- Verify the digital signature on every request using a hash.
- Validate the sender's IP address against the payment gateway's IP whitelist.

data.name:
Payment
Request
data.description: E-commerce application sends a payment processing request to the Payment Gateway via API, including amount, currency, payment method, and token

threats.title: API Request Tampering
threats.description: An attacker who has compromised the e-commerce server or is performing a man-in-the-middle attack on internal communications modifies payment requests sent to the payment gateway.
threats.mitigation:
- Use API keys and secrets with regular rotation schedules.
- Implement request signing with a hash.
- Validate request integrity on both ends.

data.name:
Request and Validate
Payment
data.description: Payment Gateway sends a payment validation and authorization request to the Bank/financial service provider

threats.title: Information Disclosure
threats.description: Sensitive transaction data transmitted between the payment gateway and the bank is exposed due to improperly configured encryption or vulnerabilities in the communication protocol.
threats.mitigation:
- Enforce end-to-end encryption on all communications.
- Use a dedicated secure network for payment gateway-to-bank communication.

data.name: Payment Gateway / Banking Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the payment gateway and the banking network

data.name: Internet / Public Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the user (public internet) and the e-commerce infrastructure

data.name: E-Commerce Internal / Payment Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the e-commerce infrastructure and the payment gateway/banking network

data.name:
Order and
Payment Detail
data.description: User submits product order data and enters payment details (payment method, card/e-wallet/bank information) to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Eavesdropping
threats.description: An attacker intercepts communication between the user's browser and the e-commerce server via a man-in-the-middle attack to steal payment data.
threats.mitigation: - Use HTTPS.

threats.title: Payment Request Tampering
threats.description: An attacker modifies payment data sent from the user to the e-commerce server.
threats.mitigation: - Validate all payment data on the backend.

data.name: Respons Validasi Pembayaran
data.description: Bank/financial service provider sends a payment validation response (approved/declined, authorization code) back to the Payment Gateway
